mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for credit spreads.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 70% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered cases during recovery phases, or return autocorrelation fully explains the pattern instead of industrial production data.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 55% of periods where CPI exceeds the threshold, or inflation fails to correlate with subsequent rate hike announcements and bond yield movements.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 60% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks and earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns across multiple business cycles, or earnings revisions fully mediate the relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding one percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets absorb the production drop without declining, yielding positive returns in more than 50% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal is subsumed by broader credit spread widening.
